title: `podman machine start` hangs indefinitely on macOS arm64 EC2 instance
body:
### Issue Description

When running `podman machine start` on a macOS arm64 EC2 instance, it prints `Starting machine "podman-machine-default"` and then hangs without doing anything. 

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. In the AWS console, go to the EC2 instance page and click Connect.
2. Use Session Manager to connect to the EC2 instance.
3. Download the installer with `curl -L -o /tmp/podman.pkg https://github.com/containers/podman/releases/download/v5.4.1/podman-installer-macos-arm64.pkg`.
5. Install podman with `sudo installer -pkg /tmp/podman.pkg -target /`.
6. Update $PATH with `export PATH=$PATH:/opt/podman/bin`.
7. Initialize the VM with `podman machine init --memory 16384 --cpus 2`.
8. Start the VM with `podman machine start`.

### Describe the results you received

Here is the output of all the commands I ran:

```bash
sh-3.2$ curl -L -o /tmp/podman.pkg https://github.com/containers/podman/releases/download/v5.4.1/podman-installer-macos-arm64.pkg
  % Total    % Received % Xferd  Average Speed   Time    Time     Time  Current
                                 Dload  Upload   Total   Spent    Left  Speed
  0     0    0     0    0     0      0      0 --:--:-- --:--:-- --:--:--     0
100 56.1M  100 56.1M    0     0  90.7M      0 --:--:-- --:--:-- --:--:-- 90.7M

sh-3.2$ sudo installer -pkg /tmp/podman.pkg -target /
installer: Package name is Podman 5.4.1
installer: Installing at base path /
installer: The install was successful.

sh-3.2$ export PATH=$PATH:/opt/podman/bin

sh-3.2$ podman machine init --memory 16384 --cpus 2
Looking up Podman Machine image at quay.io/podman/machine-os:5.4 to create VM
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 49c3b3279cd1 done   |
Copying config 44136fa355 done   |
Writing manifest to image destination
49c3b3279cd1ac106e37700a5763f4ff6302d1153745b40d8cc14750f862afce
Extracting compressed file: podman-machine-default-arm64.raw: done
Machine init complete
To start your machine run:

        podman machine start

sh-3.2$ podman machine start
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
```

From this point out, the `podman machine start` commands indefinitely until being cancelled.

### Describe the results you expected

I expected the VM to successfully start after running `podman machine start`.

### podman info output

```yaml
OS: darwin/arm64
buildOrigin: pkginstaller
provider: applehv
version: 5.4.1

Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: failed to connect: dial tcp 127.0.0.1:49351: connect: connection refused
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

AMI name: amzn-ec2-macos-15.3.1-20250314-110616-arm64
Instance type: mac2.metal

### Additional information

None

comments.body: Noticed there was a newer version, so I re-ran the same commands listed above using https://github.com/containers/podman/releases/download/v5.4.2/podman-installer-macos-arm64.pkg. Still encountering the same issue.
do you have write access in the curdir when you issue the podman comand ?
another option, do you have gui access to this instance? if so, `podman --log-level debug machine start` might reveal something in the debug console.

Our macos CI runs on mac2.metal machines using an older macos 15.1.1 based image. So I would assume the ec2 instances itself should work fine, maybe try some different image versions. Otherwise as @baude mentioned we will need to get a proper error to find out what is wrong.

> do you have write access in the curdir when you issue the podman comand ?

I do, I'm running these commands in my ssm-user's home directory.

> another option, do you have gui access to this instance? if so, podman --log-level debug machine start might reveal something in the debug console.

Sure thing, here is the output with debug logs enabled, up to the point it hangs:

> anything interesting in `/tmp/podman/podman-machine-default.log`?

After killing the `podman machine start`, the file exists but is empty.

> What's `/tmp/` usage (`df -h /tmp`)?

```
sh-3.2$ df -h /tmp
Filesystem      Size    Used   Avail Capacity iused ifree %iused  Mounted on
/dev/disk5s1   100Gi    14Gi    68Gi    18%    185k  712M    0%   /System/Volumes/Data
```

In the same `/tmp/podman` directory there is also a `gvproxy.log`:

```
time="2025-04-29T14:50:27Z" level=info msg="gvproxy version v0.8.4"
time="2025-04-29T14:50:27Z" level=info msg="waiting for clients..."
time="2025-04-29T14:54:19Z" level=error msg="gvproxy exiting: setupProxy failed: timeout: connect tcp 192.168.127.2:22: no route to host"
```
do you have write access to `curdir` when you run the podman command?

I had this problem on a arm64 Mac Mini. Using Podman Desktop instead of the CLI to create a VM prompted me to install Rosetta, after doing this the issue was resolved even when using the CLI to initialise a machine.

> I had this problem on a arm64 Mac Mini. Using Podman Desktop instead of the CLI to create a VM prompted me to install Rosetta, after doing this the issue was resolved even when using the CLI to initialise a machine.

I can confirm installing rosetta with `softwareupdate --install-rosetta` fixes this for CLI install without podman-desktop as well. Also on Mac Mini M4
Rosetta install problem is tracked in https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/23153
